mutations.description: Accept a timer period or initial compare value outside its documented bounds.
mutations.find:
period_ticks < UINT16_C(2) || period_ticks > TIMER0_MAX_PERIOD_TICKS ||
    initial_compare_ticks > period_ticks

mutations.description: Leave stale DMA terminal status uncleared during initialization.
Enable the timer before programming its initial compare shadow.
Start DMA with a compare sample above the configured timer period.
Hand timer ownership to DMA without clearing stale terminal status.
Program DMA to a non-timer destination.
Configure a descriptor but never enable the DMA channel.
Enable timer DMA requests before the DMA channel owns a valid descriptor.
Accept a new handoff while DMA or a terminal result still owns state.
Configure shared DMA ownership state without masking the IRQ.
Always restore start as interrupt-enabled rather than restoring the opaque state.
Request channel abort without first releasing timer DMA requests.
Mark an abort pending without asking DMA to terminate the transfer.
Ignore error status when multiple terminal status bits are latched.
Leave an aborted DMA transfer in its prior owner state.
Report completion without returning timer ownership to the CPU.
Attempt a terminal handoff while retaining timer DMA requests.
Lose the compare value that must be retained across abort or error recovery.
Allow recovery before the terminal result is consumed.
Reconfigure the timer with zero instead of the retained active compare value.
Finish reconfiguration without restoring CPU ownership.
Return a terminal result without clearing its one-result latch.
Publish an error result without retaining the DMA error cause.
Restore the result-consumption critical section as enabled regardless of its saved state.
